record.field: Linkwarden is a self-hosted collaborative bookmark manager that collects, organizes, reads and permanently preserves the webpages that matter to you.

Linkwarden is a self-hosted collaborative bookmark manager that collects, organizes, reads and permanently preserves the webpages that matter to you.

It is built to fight link rot: every saved link is automatically archived as a screenshot, PDF and single-file HTML, so the content survives even when the original page disappears. Beyond plain bookmarking, it adds a reader view with highlighting and annotations, organization by collections, sub-collections and tags, and collaboration so teams can curate shared link libraries. It positions itself as a self-hosted alternative to cloud services like Raindrop.io.

Key features
  • Automatic web archiving of each link as screenshot, PDF and HTML, plus optional Wayback Machine snapshots
  • Reader view with full-text search, highlighting and annotations
  • Organization by collections, sub-collections, nested folders and multiple tags
  • AI-based automatic tagging that classifies links by their content
  • Collaborative shared collections with per-user permissions and bulk actions
  • Browser extensions, native iOS/Android apps, RSS feed subscriptions and SSO integration
Best for

Individuals, researchers and teams who want a privacy-respecting, self-hosted home for their bookmarks and reading material, with durable archiving and shared curation instead of relying on a third-party cloud service.

field.name: tag_ids
field.eval: [Command.set([ref('agz_infra_suite.tag_self_hosted'), ref('agz_infra_suite.tag_productivity'), ref('agz_infra_suite.tag_collaboration'), ref('agz_infra_suite.tag_documentation'), ref('agz_infra_suite.tag_search'), ref('agz_infra_suite.tag_ai'), ref('agz_infra_suite.tag_bookmarks')])]

record.field: services: linkwarden: environment: - NEXTAUTH_SECRET - NEXTAUTH_URL - DATABASE_URL=postgresql://linkwarden:{{ postgres_password }}@postgres:5432/linkwarden restart: unless-stopped image: ghcr.io/linkwarden/linkwarden:v2.9.3 ports: - '{{ PORT_HTTP }}:3000' volumes: - ./linkwarden_data:/data/data depends_on: - postgres healthcheck: test: curl --fail http://localhost:3000 || exit 1 interval: 60s retries: 2 start_period: 60s timeout: 15s container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}' networks: - internal - agz-proxy-network postgres: image: postgres:17-alpine restart: unless-stopped user: postgres environment: POSTGRES_USER: linkwarden POSTGRES_DB: linkwarden POSTGRES_PASSWORD: {{ postgres_password }} volumes: - ./postgres_data:/var/lib/postgresql/data healthcheck: test: - CMD-SHELL - pg_isready interval: 10s timeout: 5s retries: 5 container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}_postgres' networks: - internal networks: internal: driver: bridge agz-proxy-network: external: true

#!/bin/bash set -euo pipefail cd "$SERVICE_PATH" # Dump consistente de PostgreSQL (servicio en marcha) → $BACKUP_DATA_DIR docker compose exec -T postgres sh -c 'pg_dump -U "$POSTGRES_USER" -d "$POSTGRES_DB" --no-owner -Fc' \ > "$BACKUP_DATA_DIR/db.dump" echo "pg_dump OK ($(wc -c < "$BACKUP_DATA_DIR/db.dump") bytes)"

#!/bin/bash set -euo pipefail cd "$SERVICE_PATH" # Levanta solo la DB, espera readiness y restaura docker compose up -d postgres for i in $(seq 1 30); do docker compose exec -T postgres sh -c 'pg_isready -U "$POSTGRES_USER"' && break sleep 2 done docker compose exec -T postgres sh -c 'pg_restore -U "$POSTGRES_USER" -d "$POSTGRES_DB" --clean --if-exists --no-owner' \ < "$RESTORE_DATA_DIR/db.dump" # Verificación: la DB no quedó vacía (sh -c con dobles comillas; $VAR del contenedor con \$) N=$(docker compose exec -T postgres sh -c "psql -U \$POSTGRES_USER -d \$POSTGRES_DB -tAc \"SELECT count(*) FROM information_schema.tables WHERE table_schema='public'\"") echo "tablas restauradas: $N" test "$N" -gt 0
